stem: A HR analytics study surveys 15 staff members and observes 10 positive outcomes out of 15 (66.7%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (5 observations, rate = 60.0%) and Group B (10 observations, rate = 70.0%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 43% and 56% of the population. Estimate the true employee turnover rate and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.6667. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.6353 (range [0.6261, 0.6432]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.4172, 0.8483]. Point estimate: 0.6353.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.49: 0.49×0.568 + 0.51×0.7 = 0.6353.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=15, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
